question: Do not run commands. From the Megatron-LM linting-and-formatting skill, answer in at most 6 bullets: give the exact check-mode and fix-mode autoformat.sh commands, the import-ordering command required after editing imports, the linting dependency-group install command, and three concrete style rules from the skill.
ground_truth: The response should use the linting-and-formatting skill and include: BASE_REF=main CHECK_ONLY=true SKIP_DOCS=false bash tools/autoformat.sh for check mode; BASE_REF=main CHECK_ONLY=false bash tools/autoformat.sh for fix mode; uv run isort <file1>.py <file2>.py after editing imports; uv sync --locked --only-group linting inside the container to install ruff, black, isort, and pylint; tools invoked by autoformat include black, isort, pylint, ruff, and mypy; style rules include X | None instead of Optional[X], Google-style docstrings on public classes/functions, 119-character line length, and no bare except.
expected_behavior: Uses the linting-and-formatting skill guidance before answering.
Gives the exact autoformat.sh check-mode and fix-mode commands.
Mentions uv run isort for edited import files.
Mentions uv sync --locked --only-group linting inside the container.
Includes concrete style rules from the skill rather than generic Python style advice.
